name: Dynamic Programming
description: Dynamic programming solves optimization problems that have optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems: a recurrence relating a solution to smaller subproblems is defined and solved either top-down with memoization or bottom-up by filling a table, after which the stored results are combined and the optimal solution is reconstructed.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Optimization problem"]
    N2{"Optimal substructure?"}
    N3["Not DP-suitable"]
    N4{"Overlapping subproblems?"}
    N5["Define recurrence"]
    N6{"Top-down or bottom-up?"}
    N7["Top-down: recurse+cache"]
    N8["Bottom-up: fill table"]
    N9["Solve subproblems in order"]
    N10["Combine to optimal"]
    N11["Reconstruct solution"]
    N12["Optimal answer"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|no| N3
    N3 -->|alt| N12
    N2 -->|yes| N4
    N4 -->|no| N3
    N4 -->|yes| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|top-down| N7
    N6 -->|bottom-up| N8
    N7 --> N9
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

sources.title: Introduction to Algorithms (4th ed.)
sources.authors: Cormen, T. H.; Leiserson, C. E.; Rivest, R. L.; Stein, C.
